chore: update dependabot config to ignore @types/node major versions (#1295)

`@types/node` major versions track Node.js major versions. Allowing
dependabot to bump `@types/node` across majors introduces type
definitions for a runtime the project doesn't develop against — silently
expanding the apparent API surface and risking broken builds when Node
removes or renames APIs.

## Changes

- **`.github/dependabot.yml`**: Added an `ignore` rule for `@types/node`
scoped to `version-update:semver-major` in the `npm` ecosystem block.
Minor and patch updates continue to flow through normally.


No QA Required

commit.message:
perf(maglev): elide MOVSXD for chained smi_guarded operations

Extend narrow_int32 analysis to recognize smi_guarded Generic arithmetic
ops (GenericAdd/Sub/Mul/Inc/Dec/Negate) as both 32-bit producers and
consumers. When a smi_guarded op's result feeds only other smi_guarded
ops (or narrow-transparent Phis), the intermediate MOVSXD sign-extension
is skipped — the next 32-bit ALU instruction only reads lower 32 bits.

For a chain of 5 GenericAdd ops (like property_access benchmark), this
eliminates 4 out of 5 MOVSXDs — only the final result before Return
needs sign-extension.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(maglev): use 32-bit ops + JO for smi_guarded overflow detection

Replace MOVSXD+CMP+JNE overflow checks with 32-bit ADD/SUB/IMUL + JO
(overflow flag) for all smi_guarded arithmetic operations:

- GenericAdd: add32_rr + JO + MOVSXD (was: add_rr + MOVSXD + CMP + JNE)
- GenericSub: sub32_rr + JO + MOVSXD
- GenericMul: imul32_rr + JO + MOVSXD
- Inc: add32_ri + JO + MOVSXD
- Dec: sub32_ri + JO + MOVSXD
- Negate: sub32_rr + JO + MOVSXD

Saves 1 instruction per smi_guarded arithmetic op (6 instead of 7).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: derive cookie Secure flag from base URL, not NODE_ENV

When running in production over HTTP (e.g. LXC on local network),
NODE_ENV=production was setting the Secure cookie flag, causing the
browser to refuse sending the session cookie over plain HTTP. This
broke the OAuth state validation (state_mismatch error).

Now the Secure flag is set only when NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL starts
with https://, which correctly handles HTTP-in-production setups.

fix: shorten newsletter URL params to stay under Mailgun 1000-char tracking limit

Mailgun silently skips click-tracking for href values >= 1000 characters.
Our newsletter URLs with authToken JWT were ~1010-1087 chars, causing
only 7/24 links to be tracked (those with shorter paths).

Changes:
- newsletter_email -> ne (saves 14 chars)
- authToken -> at (saves 7 chars)
- Drop newsletter_autologin param (always 1, saves 24 chars)
- Drop newsletter_source param (always 'weekly', saves 26 chars)
- Drop subscriber_key param (redundant with email, saves 40 chars)
- Revert & encoding (not the root cause)
- Total savings: ~111 chars -> max URL now ~976 chars

Client-side (App.tsx, authService.ts, build-plugins) reads both old
and new param names for backwards compatibility with cached emails.

commit.message:
perf(maglev): speculative Smi guard for LoadNamedGeneric arithmetic chains

Add compute_smi_guarded() analysis that identifies LoadNamedGeneric nodes
whose results feed exclusively into arithmetic operations (GenericAdd/Sub/
Mul/Inc/Dec/Negate/bitwise). These nodes get a Smi deopt guard at their
load site (MOVSXD+CMP+JNE deopt) and downstream arithmetic consumers skip
per-operand Smi checks entirely.

Key changes:
- New smi_guarded set (superset of i32_range) with consumer-based filtering
- Fixed-point propagation through arithmetic ops and Phi nodes
- LoadNamedGeneric emits Smi guard when in smi_guarded set
- GenericAdd/Sub/Mul: 7 instructions (was 13) with smi_guarded inputs
- Inc/Dec/Negate: 6-7 instructions with smi_guarded inputs
- Bitwise ops: 4 instructions (same as i32_range, no overflow possible)

Expected impact on property_access: 5x7=35 instructions/iter vs 5x13=65
(~46% reduction in loop body size).

commit.message:
fix: thread safety, NVS wear, blocking delay, devirtualization

Song title race: Replace single song_title_ buffer with double-buffer
pattern (title_bufs_[2]). Core 0 audio callbacks write to the inactive
buffer then atomically flip title_read_idx_. Core 1 always reads from
the stable buffer, eliminating torn reads without a mutex.

Station ordering race: Copy station URL into pending_url_ buffer before
setting pending_connect_ flag. Core 0 audio task reads pending_url_
directly instead of dereferencing stations_[current_station_], which
could read a stale index if Core 1 changed it between flag set and
consumption.

Non-blocking PA enable: Replace 200ms delay() in connect_station_()
with a deferred state machine. pa_pending_ flag and pa_pending_ms_
timestamp are set on station connect; loop() enables the PA pin after
200ms without blocking the ESPHome main loop.

Debounced NVS saves: Volume changes from HA (set/up/down), touch
slider, and touch buttons all use mark_vol_dirty_() instead of
immediate NVS writes. flush_vol_if_dirty_() in loop() commits to
flash only after 3s of inactivity, preventing flash wear from rapid
volume adjustments. Also fixes set_volume_direct() (touch slider)
which previously never persisted volume to NVS.

Devirtualization: Mark InternetRadio, I2SBridge, WinampDisplay as
final classes, enabling compiler to eliminate vtable lookups for
loop(), setup(), get_setup_priority() per ESPHome 2026.3.0 guidance.
